seoTitle: How to Export Zendesk Tickets to CSV, JSON, or XML
metaDescription: Export your full Zendesk ticket history for backup, migration, or analysis using account-wide exports in CSV, JSON, or XML format via the Admin Center.
intro: Whether you're backing up your data or migrating to a new platform, Zendesk lets you export your full ticket history rather than relying on manual copying. This guide walks through enabling account exports, choosing the right file format, setting a date range, and retrieving your download link.

steps.heading: Request export access
steps.body: Contact Zendesk support first, since account-wide data exports need to be enabled before you can use them.

steps.heading: Open the reporting tools
steps.body: In the Admin Center, go to Account Settings and select Tools Reports to find the export options.

steps.heading: Choose your file format
steps.body: Select the Export tab and pick CSV for lightweight spreadsheets or JSON/XML for more structured, script-ready data.

steps.heading: Set your date range
steps.body: Apply historical date filters to scope exactly which ticket lifecycles you want included.

steps.heading: Trigger the export
steps.body: Click Export to start the cloud processing sequence for your archive.

steps.heading: Check your email for the download link
steps.body: Once processing finishes, check your admin email inbox for the secure system link to download your file.

faq.question: Why can't I export tickets right away?
faq.answer: Zendesk requires an initial activation step from their support team before account-wide exports become available.

faq.question: Which format should I choose for a large ticket history?
faq.answer: JSON is typically better for large or script-ready datasets, while CSV works well for straightforward spreadsheet analysis.

faq.question: Why does the standard view download time out on big exports?
faq.answer: Standard view-based downloads have a time limit, which is why running an account-wide background export avoids that timeout.

faq.question: How long does an export usually take to process?
faq.answer: Processing time depends on the size of your ticket history, so check your email periodically rather than waiting on the page.
